title: CryptoCb uses process-global AtomicBool not a Client lifetime borrow
description: cryptocb.rs uses a process-global AtomicBool (REGISTERED) rather than tying CryptoCbGuard's lifetime to &mut Client. The wolfCrypt device table is a C process-global, so exclusion must also be process-global — a Client lifetime borrow would only prevent double-registration from one Client instance, not across two separate Clients. The AtomicBool correctly enforces the invariant across all instances. A future contributor might suggest a lifetime-bound CryptoCbGuard<'client> to avoid the global, but that would be false safety. SeqCst ordering is also correct: the compare_exchange must order before the C call on all platforms.
